name: Finding and Evaluating Projects
description: Learn how to find your first project using discovery tools, understanding labels, and identifying beginner-friendly tasks.
author: being users of software they contribute to
learningObjectives:
After this lesson, the learner should be able to:
- Use discovery tools to find beginner-friendly open source projects.
- Interpret standard GitHub labels like 'good first issue'.
- Evaluate a project's health and welcoming nature for new contributors.
abstract: This resource gives a broad overview of terms and processes important to understand as a new contributor. It also includes suggestions on how to find a project to contribute to.
notes: Maybe not relevant for researchers, but useful for students as a self-guided entry point
